items: Client: {{fact:f:E-2017-001.client}}
Engagement kickoff: {{fact:f:E-2017-001.start}}
Prepared by Daniel Valenzuela, Advisor
With Michelle Rose, Communications Associate
Client sponsor: Mary Frank, Director of Operations
A strong operating story that is under-told outside the current investor base.
Messaging drifts across the website, recent releases, and leadership remarks.
No single sentence a reporter or analyst would reach for to describe the company.
Peers have moved faster to claim the category language the company should own.
The upside is real: the fundamentals support a sharper, more confident story.
Leadership wants growth framed around discipline, not scale for its own sake.
The board is wary of anything that reads as pre-IPO hype.
Operations sees a gap between how the company runs and how it sounds.
Investors respond to proof, so every claim needs a fact standing behind it.
Broad agreement that the current boilerplate has aged out.
Settle a single core positioning statement before anything else moves.
Rebuild the messaging map around audience, claim, and proof.
Align the executive narrative so leaders tell one consistent story.
Retire the language that reads as promotional and replace it with evidence.
Order the announcement calendar behind the new frame.
Daniel Valenzuela leads the engagement and owns client review.
Michelle Rose drafts the messaging map and routes each version for approval.
Status runs by email each week; decisions are confirmed in writing.
Nothing external goes out ahead of Mary Frank and the {{fact:f:E-2017-001.client}} leadership review.
Circulate the draft positioning statement for comment this week.
Book a working session to pressure-test the messaging map together.
Collect the outstanding proof points from the {{fact:f:E-2017-001.client}} team.
Confirm the near-term announcement we are writing toward.

blocks.text: Thanks for turning the materials around so quickly. Read against the kickoff notes, they confirm we are aligned on where the {{fact:f:E-2017-001.client}} story needs to land, and the gaps you flagged are the ones we want to close first.

Michelle Rose is folding your comments into the working draft now. One ask before it travels any further: let's hold the investor-facing lines until the internal narrative is locked, so nothing circulates ahead of your leadership review.
Could we get thirty minutes with the three of us this week to walk the messaging map? Send me a couple of windows that work on your side and I will set it up.
Following up on Daniel Valenzuela's note. The revised messaging map is ready in the version he and I talked through, and I will send it over this afternoon.

What changed: the core statement is tighter, each claim now sits over its proof point, and I pulled the two lines that read as too promotional for the board. What is still open: I would like your read on whether the growth language goes far enough, and there is one proof point only the {{fact:f:E-2017-001.client}} operations team can confirm.
If you can get me edits early next week, I will have a clean version back before we meet. Happy to jump on the call Daniel suggested whenever it lands on the calendar.

Thanks for the quick edits and for the steer on the growth language. I agree with the direction: lead with disciplined growth and let the scale numbers support it rather than carry it. That reads as credible to the board and still gives the {{fact:f:E-2017-001.client}} story room to grow into.

Michelle Rose will lock the core positioning statement against your comments and reconcile the one open proof point with your operations team. Once that is settled, everything downstream lines up cleanly.

The piece I want to get on the calendar is the working session to pressure-test the full map together. Could you confirm who from your side should be in the room, and I will propose a couple of dates?
Quick note to close the loop from Daniel Valenzuela's message. The core positioning statement is locked, and I have folded your last round of edits into the map, so what you have now is current.
The only item still moving is the proof point on the operations side. Once your team confirms the figure, that claim is finished and the {{fact:f:E-2017-001.client}} map is complete for this phase.

For the working session, could you let me know who should join and whether mornings or afternoons are easier? I will send a short agenda ahead of time so we stay focused. Daniel and I will come ready to walk each section and take decisions in the room.

items: Daniel Valenzuela, Advisor
Michelle Rose, Communications Associate
Mary Frank, Director of Operations, {{fact:f:E-2017-001.client}}

blocks.text: The session took stock of the positioning work at the midpoint of the engagement. Daniel Valenzuela opened by walking the group through the core positioning statement, now settled, and the messaging map that sits beneath it. Mary Frank confirmed that the disciplined-growth framing reflects how leadership at {{fact:f:E-2017-001.client}} wants the company described, and that the board's reaction to the earlier draft had been encouraging.

Michelle Rose reviewed the map claim by claim. Most proof points are confirmed; the one open item on the operations side was resolved during the meeting when Mary Frank supplied the missing figure. The group agreed the map is complete for the current phase and that attention now shifts to sequencing, aligning the executive narrative and ordering the announcement calendar behind the new frame. Daniel Valenzuela flagged one risk to watch: with the statement settled, the temptation is to start using the new language externally before leadership has fully absorbed it, which is exactly the sequence the engagement is meant to prevent.

Discussion then turned to the next phase. Mary Frank raised the concern that internal and external messaging stay in step as the {{fact:f:E-2017-001.client}} team begins using the new language day to day. Daniel Valenzuela proposed a short internal rollout note for leadership before anything external moves, and Michelle Rose agreed to draft it. The group confirmed that no external communications will go out until leadership has signed off on the internal language.

On cadence, the group agreed to keep the weekly status thread running and to reconvene for a full working session once the rollout note has been reviewed. Mary Frank asked that any interim decisions be confirmed in writing so the operations team is never guessing at the current version. Michelle Rose will keep the master copy of the messaging map and note the date of each change, and Daniel Valenzuela will bring a first cut of the announcement sequence to the next session for the group to react to.

rows: Finalize the messaging map and circulate the clean version
Draft the internal rollout note for leadership review
Confirm leadership sign-off on the internal language
Sequence the announcement calendar against the new frame
Reconfirm the remaining proof points with the operations team
